title: ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - 211info
text: LATINO NETWORK ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with electric bills, natural gas bills, and heating fuel. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: 60% of State Median Income for household size (including gross income for all members of household ages 18 and older). Certain kinds of energy assistance may have additional requirements, including age restrictions and the need for a Social Security number. Hours: Not accepting appointments Email (503) 208-2174 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 283-6881 Main phone Energy Line (503) 208-2174 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to check for available appointments. If appointments are not available, individuals are advised to check back the next day. No walk-in appointments. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: -Photo ID for all persons in the home age 18 or older -Social Security cards for anyone in the home who has one -Current utility bill -Income verification, such as a 30 day pay stub, Social Security benefits, TANF, pension, child support, unemployment etc. LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Electric Service Payment Assistance Electric Service Payment Assistance * Hispanic/Latino Community Gas Service Payment Assistance Gas Service Payment Assistance * Hispanic/Latino Community Heating Fuel Payment Assistance Heating Fuel Payment Assistance * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADVOCACY EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS AL KINDER EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS APRENDEMOS EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - COLEGIO DE PADRES/PARENTS COLLEGE EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ESCALERA EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - ESCALERA INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CRECIENDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - MILPA GUN VIOLENCE PREVENTION PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PADRINOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SOÑANDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - UNIDOS FOR OREGON LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - COMMUNITY HEALING INITIATIVE LEGAL SERVICES - PRESCHOOL FOR ALL NAVIGATOR SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CONEXIONES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - NINTH GRADE COUNTS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - P3 COORDINATORS UTILITIES - CITY OF PORTLAND WATER DISCOUNT PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LATINO NETWORK
